body:
Here’s why I’m writing to you before I meet with him. |

Friend, I meet with Mr. Trump every week to review our fundraising numbers. I provide him with a list of our newest donors, the number of contributions we received, our average contribution, and how much more we need to raise to stay on budget. He loves seeing the tremendous support we get from patriots across the country. He’s blown away by the small-dollar contributions. Those are his favorite. He even talks about them in his rallies. Our numbers this week are good so far, but not nearly as high as previous weeks. Friend: I’m turning to you to help us change that. Please make a contribution of $100, $65, $50, $35, $25, or $10 before the meeting. We’re now facing the most critical moment of our campaign. We only have 25 days left until every vote is cast and the election is over. Hillary’s special interests are spending millions of dollars to elect her, because they’re scared to death of a Trump White House. They know he’s determined to end the lobbyist gravy train and end the corrupt business of trading political favors for cash. But the only way we can win is if the American people rise up and take the place of the special interests that typically support presidential campaigns. Our campaign is different. It’s run by the people -- and it will win by the people. This is your chance to step up and make a contribution to the only campaign in history that is truly FOR, BY, and OF the American people. Please make a contribution of $100, $65, $50, $35, $25, or $10 before I meet with Mr. Trump to show him our latest fundraising numbers. Thank you, Brad Parscale Digital Director CONTRIBUTE $100

subject: Only 4 weeks left
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donald J
body:
Tell your neighbors to vote Trump. |

Friend, It’s crunch time. With election day less than 4 weeks away, it is crucial that every American hears about my father’s vision for making America great again before casting their votes. I only have one question for you: Are you all in, Friend? With all the generous donations so far, we have been able to put commercials and advertisements on the air to spread the word. But these aren’t nearly enough. TV commercials, speeches and ad campaigns can’t replace face-to-face conversations. They can’t persuade people to go to the polls; only supporters like you can do that. We won’t win this without you, Friend. You help start this movement and you have been there every step of the way. Now we need you to step up and help us finish strong. This weekend volunteers all over the country have committed to knock 100 doors in their neighborhoods, talk to their friends and neighbors and make sure they know how we are going to Make America Great Again. Will you join them? This is YOUR time. This is YOUR year. This is do or die. It’s time to win again. Will you commit to knocking on 100 doors this weekend? With your help, we can make sure that Crooked Hillary never gets to the White House. Thank you! Eric Trump Yes! I'll Knock 100 Doors

body:
_This is our Link of the Week email -- the things that we're sharing around the office, and we think everyone on Team Maggie should know about. We hope you'll enjoy clicking around as much as we do._
After deciding that voting for Donald Trump would derail her chances of keeping her seat, Kelly Ayotte announced that she will instead write-in Trump's running mate, Indiana Governor Mike Pence, for President -- as if he's a better choice to run our country.
Under Pence's leadership, Indiana has legalized discrimination against the LGBTQ community and slashed Planned Parenthood funding. Mike Marland at The Concord Monitor nailed it with this cartoon about how Ayotte's political calculations don't add up:
[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fda0a/2a380d31/163368db/fcadae/576138417/VEsH/ "https://twitter.com/marlandcartoons/status/785863373447630848")
_Mike Marland, Concord Monitor. Discovered via[Twitter.](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fda0a/2a380d31/163368db/fcadae/576138417/VEsE/ "https://twitter.com/marlandcartoons/status/785863373447630848")_
Now we know where Kelly Ayotte _really_ stands on the issues.
-- Team Maggie
P.S. -- If we don't take back the Senate, Washington Republicans could confirm multiple Trump/Pence Supreme Court nominees, and our country would suffer the consequences for _decades._
But here's the good news -- we have a chance to stop this from happening. We hope you'll enjoy clicking around as much as we do. After deciding that voting for Donald Trump would derail her chances of keeping her seat, Kelly Ayotte announced that she will instead write-in Trump's running mate, Indiana Governor Mike Pence, for President -- as if he's a better choice to run our country. Under Pence's leadership, Indiana has legalized discrimination against the LGBTQ community and slashed Planned Parenthood funding. Mike Marland at The Concord Monitor nailed it with this cartoon about how Ayotte's political calculations don't add up: "" Mike Marland, Concord Monitor. Friend,

From the launch of his campaign for governor, Greg Gianforte made the calculation that he couldn't raise enough money to beat Steve.

That's because our grassroots supporters continue to step up time and time again. **[Will you chip in $15?](http://salsa4.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=D39%2Fis8rxHcbCbqIn7Hci6encr47Kv6U)**

Instead, Mr. Gianforte decided to pour in money from his own bank account -- $3.3 million so far, an amount unheard of since the days of the Copper Kings.

**He is spending his massive fortune on smoke and mirrors -- trying to distract from his lobbying for a statewide sales tax, outsourcing American jobs and the lawsuit he filed to block public stream access next to his mansion.**

It's the problem with self-funding. Greg Gianforte is banking on being able to spend enough money that he'll never have to answer to the voters of our state. We hope you won't allow that to happen.

**Our campaign is grassroots-funded and Steve is accountable to Montanans.[Please make this matter and contribute $15.](http://salsa4.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=eLFlN0P7DIyxspDlzJYpOemcFipf01EL)**

Thanks,

name: =?utf-8?Q?Ryan=20Manion?=
subject: We Need to Have Eric Greitens' Back
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greitens for Missouri, Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer
body:
Peter,

My brother Travis was a Marine. He served with Eric Greitens in Iraq. Eric and Travis woke up one night to the sound of mortars and loud explosions. It didn't take long to realize that it was a coordinated attack. Then suddenly, a suicide truck bomb packed with poisonous chlorine gas rolled into the site and blew.

As chaos unfolded below and injured soldiers were evacuated, Travis and Eric climbed up to the roof to provide overwatch for the men below. Eric asked Travis, "You got it?" Travis looked at Eric and told him, "I've got your back."

That was the last thing my brother ever said to Eric. Three weeks later he was killed by an enemy sniper.
[](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=f9473365b8&e=84041254e6)
Leaders like Eric Greitens are rare. His opponent is right, he's not a politician. He is a fearless leader--one who has shown my family an incredible amount of love after my brother's sacrifice.

My brother had Eric's back in Iraq. If he were here today, he'd have it again on this mission. That's why all of us need to [step up](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=d586639436&e=84041254e6) and [do whatever we can](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=6fa2b6ceec&e=84041254e6) to have Eric's back on this mission to bring high-paying jobs, better schools, and safer streets to all Missouri families.

Please, [join me on this mission](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=aa183c0b3a&e=84041254e6) to have Eric's back. We need leaders with the courage of Travis and Eric to get our country back on track.

Best,
Ryan Manion
Paid for by Greitens for Missouri, Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer
4579 Laclede Avenue #138
St. Louis, Missouri 63108
Eric is a former member of the United States Navy having served four deployments overseas. Use of his military rank, titles and photographs in uniform does not imply an endorsement from the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.
This email was sent to dpwillis67+greitens@gmail.com. You can also follow us on [Facebook](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=5208c0479c&e=84041254e6) and [Twitter](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=6ac3f0c61c&e=84041254e6). Emails are one of the best ways to stay connected with the campaign. If they become too much, you can [unsubscribe](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=3f71c40094&e=84041254e6&c=dbd2cc78c8), but please let us know how we can improve. You can always reach us at [team@ericgreitens.com](mailto:team@ericgreitens.com) or (314) 899-0288. You're still reading the fineprint? You must be a detail oriented, all-around awesome person who is a big supporter — you should [volunteer](http://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=031171c15b&e=84041254e6) with us!

name: Chris Koster
subject: Closed on Fridays
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Koster for Missouri, Gary Mallory, Treasurer
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/4544680245613430784/-2285715848485795840?utm_campaign=fnd_friday_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=koster&refcode=em16_161014_fnd_gq_friday_ful__ck)

Hey,

Today is Friday.

While students from Maine to Montana are busy learning, schools in 18 Missouri districts are closed. These school districts were forced to reduce their class opportunities to only offer a four-day school week, because legislators slashed the school funding formula.

And now, Eric Greitens says he's willing to cut school funding even more.

As Governor, I'll fully fund our public schools and keep the promise we've made to Missouri's children.

[**Hear more about my commitment to Missouri public schools in our latest campaign video:**](https://act.myngp.com/el/4544680245613430784/-2285715848485795840?utm_campaign=fnd_friday_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=koster&refcode=em16_161014_fnd_gq_friday_ful__ck)

**[](https://act.myngp.com/el/4544680245613430784/-2285715848485795840?utm_campaign=fnd_friday_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=koster&refcode=em16_161014_fnd_gq_friday_ful__ck)**

After the General Assembly underfunded Missouri's public schools for years, they took it even further during the 2016 legislative session -- cutting over $450 million from the state's K-12 funding formula.

Educators know that as Governor, I'll make investing in and upgrading our K-12 schools a top priority. That's why more than 35,000 public school teachers, librarians, counselors, coaches, and school psychologists across Missouri have endorsed my candidacy.

[**Watch the new video now to hear more**](https://act.myngp.com/el/4544680245613430784/-2285715848485795840?utm_campaign=fnd_friday_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=koster&refcode=em16_161014_fnd_gq_friday_ful__ck).

Thank you,

Chris Koster

**[_P.S. Help us share this video with undecided voters across the state by making a contribution today._](https://act.myngp.com/el/4544680245613430784/-2285715848485795840?utm_campaign=fnd_friday_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=koster&refcode=em16_161014_fnd_gq_friday_ful__ck)**

Paid for by Koster for Missouri, Gary Mallory, Treasurer

body:
We have three weeks left. [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=1&ms=OTY4NzYyNAS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTAyMTA0ODE5MwS2&mt=1&rt=0)
| Dear Friend, I'm crunching the numbers again. As you can imagine, there's tremendous demand on our resources right now. With just over three weeks left until Election Day -- and ballots hitting mailboxes in just a few days -- it's full steam ahead. Our opponent is up with deceptive new ads, so I need to make sure we have enough to bolster our field operations and outreach to grassroots supporters. Our field team -- along with scores of enthusiastic volunteers -- are working 24/7 to make sure our supporters vote and return their ballots. **We can 't let our field team down in these final critical weeks. If we don't turn out our voters, we lose. Period.** [**That 's why I'm asking you right up front to make a donation so our GOTV operation is second to none. Can you help us?**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=1&ms=OTY4NzYyNAS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTAyMTA0ODE5MwS2&mt=1&rt=0) We've set an ambitious goal: raising $15,000 by midnight tomorrow. And while it's ambitious, it's what we need right now to make sure Republican Bill Bryant and the Republican machine don't beat us in the field -- especially while they're running untrue ads on TV. That would be disastrous. [**Will you pitch in?**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=1&ms=OTY4NzYyNAS2&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTAyMTA0ODE5MwS2&mt=1&rt=0) Thanks, Tracy Newman

name: Corry Bliss
subject: BREAKING: Rob wins first debate!
body:
| Peter \--

Rob and failed former Governor Ted Strickland just finished their first debate -- and Rob was the clear winner!

Today’s debate highlighted the differences between Rob and Ted Strickland. Rob is getting results for Ohio, passing over 45 bills into law, including efforts to combat Ohio’s addiction epidemic and fight back against human trafficking.

On the other hand, Ted Strickland has the worst record of any candidate in America -- that's why Democrats abandoned his campaign, canceling over $20 million in TV ads for him. Ted failed to pass a single bill into law during his 12 years in Congress, and Ohio lost more than 350,000 jobs and ranked 48th in job creation on his watch as governor. There’s a reason the Ohio Teamsters, the Ohio FOP, and the United Mine Workers endorsed Rob after previously supporting Ted: Ohio simply cannot afford a return to Retread Ted’s Ohio.

With just a few weeks left until Election Day, we must keep working hard to make sure we win our race and help keep the Republican Senate Majority.

Sincerely,

Corry Bliss [](https://www.robportman.com/donate?e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=post_debate1&n=1 "Donate here!") name: Heidi =?UTF-8?Q?Sieck=2C=20=23VOTEPROCHOICE?=
subject: End Assault. End Sexism. #VOTEPROCHOICE
body:
End Assault. End Sexism. #VOTEPROCHOICE | | | [](http://democrats.ontraport.com/c/s/EFw/sBe1I/6/Y/r5cs/6FCWfE/z0PIK0OHSx) |
---|---
| | Dear Activist,

The tape of Donald Trump bragging about sexual assault has transformed the political climate right in front of our eyes. [Let’s not miss this.](http://democrats.ontraport.com/c/s/EFw/sBe1I/s/6a/r5cs/6FCWfE/k4Zq8CK86 "Link: http://voteprochoice.us/#voter-guide")

When Kelly Oxford tweeted the story of her own first assault and invited other women to join her, over 30 million women (and counting) responded with their own stories and the hashtag [#NotOkay](http://democrats.ontraport.com/c/s/EFw/sBe1I/v/69/r5cs/6FCWfE/UrU7lXnQIB).
|
---|---
| | [](http://democrats.ontraport.com/c/s/EFw/sBe1I/z/6a/r5cs/6FCWfE/b37PBeXzV) |
---|---
|
Why? Because nearly every
woman in America has
experienced sexual assault in
some form - often as a child -
and there is very little effective
recourse for the humiliation,
anger, and feeling of
powerlessness.

|
---|---
|
|
|
---|---
| | [BUT IF WE ALL VOTE TOGETHER TO REJECT SEXUAL ASSAULT AND
ALL FORMS OF SEXISM, WE CAN WIN THIS FIGHT -
NOT IN 100 YEARS, BUT ON NOVEMBER 8.](http://democrats.ontraport.com/c/s/EFw/sBe1I/U/6a/r5cs/6FCWfE/saFWhMiX1N "Link: http://voteprochoice.us/#voter-guide") |
---|---
This election is proving that women are not powerless. In fact, women, and the men who support us, are the most powerful force in America.

Women elected Barack Obama twice. And women now support Hillary Clinton by a landslide margin of 61% to 28% - that's a huge 33% lead. Even though a majority of men support Trump, women will defeat him.

But it's not enough to defeat Donald Trump. We must also defeat every politician who sides with Trump that women's bodies belong to them. |
| |
---|---
| | |
---|---|---
| |
---|---
| | That's why we created the [#VOTEPROCHOICE Voter Guide](http://democrats.ontraport.com/c/s/EFw/sBe1I/j/6a/r5cs/6FCWfE/vxmFgqyCTU "Link: http://voteprochoice.us/#voter-guide")[ ](http://democrats.ontraport.com/c/s/EFw/sBe1I/5/6a/r5cs/6FCWfE/ImVe5sJZT "Link: http://voteprochoice.us/#voter-guide") \- to help you identify all the politicians who want to outlaw abortion and send women and our doctors to prison for murder.

The issue of sexual assault and reproductive rights are one and the same.
|
---|---
|
| [](http://democrats.ontraport.com/c/s/EFw/sBe1I/r/6a/r5cs/6FCWfE/v8bcFi8EcZ) |
---|---
| | Our common enemies are the politicians who believe they have the right to control women's bodies - whether they want to assault one woman or deny all women our right to choose.

At its root, this is a fight against sexism \- hatred of and discrimination against women. And it's a fight against patriarchy \- the belief that men have the right to rule the world.

It's a fight we can win - if we join together to [#VOTEPROCHOICE](http://democrats.ontraport.com/c/s/EFw/sBe1I/Y/6a/r5cs/6FCWfE/vgAeOsQ6hK "Link: http://voteprochoice.us/#voter-guide").

Will you help us change the world?

Heidi Sieck
Co-Founder, #VOTEPROCHOICE

P.S. #VOTEPROCHOICE is a radically inclusive political engagement platform that uses the term "women " or "woman" to include both individuals who identify as women, and individuals who were assigned female at birth.
|
---|---

name: Bernie Sanders
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY OUR REVOLUTION
body:
#
Sisters and Brothers,
The disastrous decisions of the Supreme Court in the Citizens United case and related cases are undermining the very foundations of American democracy and allowing billionaires and their super PACs to buy politicians and elections.
In essence, the Citizens United ruling handed millionaires and billionaires -- who have already rigged the economy -- unlimited influence in American elections. It gave billionaires like the Koch Brothers and Sheldon Adelson the opportunity to spend hundreds of millions of dollars to elect candidates from the White House the state legislatures who will represent their interests. In essence, Citizens United is moving us toward an oligarchic form of society in which a handful of billionaires will control the economy and political life.
_The Washington Post_ , in an excellent article, showed us how far the country is moving toward becoming an oligarchy. **Incredibly, just TEN donors have poured more than $1.1 BILLION into super PACs in this election alone.**
The lesson here is that the political system isn't broken, it is rigged and owned by the billionaire class. One of the best chances to un-rig the system is with ballot initiatives this November that will help get big money out of politics.
California and Washington both have ballot initiatives that will instruct their state's elected officials to do everything in their power to work to overturn Citizens United. And Howard County, Maryland, has the chance to enact a strong system of public financing to level the playing field for local elections.
If these ballot initiatives pass, they will send an unmistakable message that the American public wants to get big money out of politics. That is why they need your help today:
[**Split a $3 contribution to the campaigns for I-735 in Washington, Prop 59 in California, and Fair Elections Howard County and help these ballot initiatives win. Make your contribution now.**](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/money-in-politics?refcode=em161014-full-full "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/money-in-politics?refcode=em161014-full-full")
The need for real campaign finance reform is not a progressive issue. It is not a conservative issue. It is an American issue.
The job now is to get big money out of politics and to end voter suppression. The country has got to create a true democracy of one-person, one-vote, not billionaires buying elections.
As Abraham Lincoln reminded us more than 150 years ago, there must be a government of the people, by the people and for the people. That starts with taking the country's destiny out of the hands of the billionaires who want to buy elections.
Citizens United must be overturned to get big money out of politics. More cities, counties and states need to move toward public financing of elections that will level the playing field for people who want to run for office.
I applaud Our Revolution's work helping to get big money out of politics, and now I am asking for your help today for the ballot initiatives in California, Washington, and Maryland that, if passed, will move the country toward reclaiming American democracy from the billionaire class.
[**Please split a $3 contribution between the campaigns helping pass three ballot initiatives to get big money out of politics.**](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/money-in-politics?refcode=em161014-full-full "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/money-in-politics?refcode=em161014-full-full")
Together, the American people can show the political establishment that it's possible to win elections powered by ordinary Americans, not millionaires, billionaires, and their super PACs. Thank you for making this happen.
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders
**[CONTRIBUTE](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/money-in-politics?refcode=em161014-full-full "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/money-in-politics?refcode=em161014-full-full")**

name: Evan Bayh
subject: Reaching out on a Friday afternoon
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Evan Bayh Committee, 133 W
body:
| [ ](https://bayh.bsd.net/page/m/32a50552/7be36b49/1e5ae126/78f81c5c/619143247/VEsH/ "http://www.evanbayhforindiana.com/")
---
Friend,

I've been pretty busy on the campaign trail this week, but I wanted to share three quick thoughts on our race:
1. **This race is truly competitive.** In fact, everyone from _USA Today_ to political expert Larry Sabato is calling it a tossup. That means it's anyone's game. **So we need to be fighting harder than the other side for the remaining 25 days.**

**[Will you help us fight harder by pitching in $5 to the last mid-month goal of the election?](https://bayh.bsd.net/page/m/32a50552/7be36b49/1e5ae126/78f81c23/619143247/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/eb-octmid-month?refcode=20161014.2nd&amount=10")**
2. My opponent's positions -- including ending Social Security as we know it and rewarding companies that ship jobs overseas -- are much too extreme for Indiana. **The stakes in this race are incredibly high.**

3. Out-of-state special interests, including the Koch brothers, are in Indiana with their deceptive ads and field teams. They intend to swing this race in my opponent's favor. **We can 't let them.**

**[Friend, will you help my team hit our $30,000 mid-month goal to finish this race strong?](https://bayh.bsd.net/page/m/32a50552/7be36b49/1e5ae126/78f81c22/619143247/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/eb-octmid-month?refcode=20161014.2nd&amount=25")**

That's what this boils down to. This race is competitive, the stakes are high, and the other side has lots of help.

**Luckily, I have you. And I 've never needed you more.**

**[Will you chip in $5 before tomorrow's mid-month deadline to make sure the candidate who best represents Hoosiers wins in November?](https://bayh.bsd.net/page/m/32a50552/7be36b49/1e5ae126/78f81c23/619143247/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/eb-octmid-month?refcode=20161014.2nd&amount=10")**

Thank you for everything,

Evan ********[Contribute](https://bayh.bsd.net/page/m/32a50552/7be36b49/1e5ae126/78f81c22/619143247/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/eb-octmid-month?refcode=20161014.2nd&amount=25")********
Paid for by the Evan Bayh Committee, 133 W. Market Street #153, Indianapolis, IN 46204

* * *

This email was sent to:
dpwillis67@gmail.com

[Unsubscribe](https://bayh.bsd.net/page/m/32a50552/7be36b49/1e5ae126/78f81c21/619143247/VEsA/ "https://bayh.bsd.net/unsubscribe")
Contributions or gifts to the Evan Bayh Committee are not tax deductible.

| We have less than one month to make sure Phil is elected our next Governor and that we restore fiscal responsibility in Montpelier so that Vermont is more affordable for each of us. Thank you for all of your support, and we know we can count on you in these final weeks. Now, check out this week’s highlights from the state’s most energetic and hardworking campaign.

**DON’T FORGET, YOU CAN VOTE NOW THROUGH EARLY VOTING**
Election day on **November 8** is still 25 days away**,** but early voting is open and you can vote any time at your town clerk’s office. Click [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=4d98147836&e=19cebfe173) to find your town clerk's location or to request an absentee ballot.

Voted early for Phil? Share your support with us on Twitter and Facebook: [@PhilScott4VT](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=9946caaf6a&e=19cebfe173) and [https://www.facebook.com/PhilScottforVermont/](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=4fbf3b0d1b&e=19cebfe173)
***
**PHIL’S ECONOMIC PLAN: REVITALIZING ECONOMIC CENTERS**
Each week, we’re diving into one of the numerous proposals and ideas Phil’s put forth to grow our economy and make Vermont more affordable, as part of his comprehensive economic development plan (which you can view [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=ab52fe801b&e=19cebfe173)). This week, let’s take a look at his proposals to **revitalize our county economic centers**. What little growth we’ve seen in Vermont over the last six years has been concentrated primarily in Chittenden County and surrounding regions. **Phil wants to ensure that all regions see greater prosperity and growth**. He’s outlined a number of proposals to **give every region of the state an equal opportunity to grow and prosper**.

Some of Phil’s ideas include **strengthening Vermont’s relationship with Quebec** for greater collaboration on shared industries; **establishing high-tech outposts** ; simplifying the process for equity crowdfunding in order to **make Vermont a hotspot for crowdfunding capital investment** ; and **tripling the number of innovation centers** similar to the Vermont Center for Emerging Technologies, Rutland’s The Loft and Bennington’s The Lightning Jar. Phil will also work to **make Vermont a supply chain hub** by directing the Agency of Commerce and Community Development (ACCD) to conduct detailed supply chain research to inform a strategy that **encourages companies that supply regional business to relocate to Vermont**. Read more about these proposals [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=8faaf2325d&e=19cebfe173) (beginning on page 17).

**Photo credit: @PhilScottVT**

*** **Governor Douglas Endorses Phil**
In a television ad that came out this week, former Governor Jim Douglas voices his strong support for Phil as the next Governor of Vermont. **Gov. Douglas endorses Phil, citing his career-long commitment to working across the aisle, and his ability to connect with Vermonters.** Phil has made it a priority to always connect with as many Vermonters as possible so he can listen, learn and lead in Montpelier. That is exactly what he will do as Governor.

Gov. Douglas also highlights **Phil’s experience as a small business owner, and how that experience gives Phil a unique understanding of the impact of a struggling economy on businesses and Vermonters.** Thank you, Gov. Douglas, for supporting Phil Scott. He is the only candidate who will make the economy and affordability priorities in Montpelier, helping working families get ahead and supporting businesses to keep and grow jobs here in Vermont. View the full endorsement [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=b6fd298ee6&e=19cebfe173).
*******
**MESSAGES FROM PHIL ON THE ECONOMY AND AFFORDABILITY**
Taking part in the Vermont PBS Candidate Commentaries, Phil has detailed his commitment to focusing on the economy and affordability, and his plans to strengthen Vermont’s economy as Governor, in a series of video segments.

As you know, **Phil is the only candidate who has made the economy and affordability cornerstones of his ENTIRE campaign**. **He’s offered the most comprehensive economic growth plan in this election, by far, with over 50 proposals and ideas to strengthen Vermont’s economy, make Vermont more affordable and address some of our most serious health and safety challenges**. In the videos featured on Vermont PBS, Phil gives another glimpse into his leadership, priorities and proposals. View the video on Phil’s #1 Priority [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=e347cc5873&e=19cebfe173) and on jobs and the economy [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=543449e84a&e=19cebfe173). Like what you see in these videos? Share them with friends and family on Facebook and Twitter, and don’t forget to tag @PhilScott4VT.
*******
**TEAM SCOTT IS ON THE MOVE**
Busy week might be an understatement this week. We are grateful to have met with so many Vermonters across the state, including in **Barre** , **Burlington** , **Essex** , **Londonderry** , **Manchester** , **Middlebury** , **Rawsonville** , **Rutland** , **Stowe** and **Waterbury**. We also got to visit **Mrs. Murphy’s** , **Woodridge Nursing Home** , **Super Thin Saws** , **Washington County Family Center** and **Burr & Burton Academy**, and met with the **Vermont Bar Association** and the **Burlington Young Professionals**. Thank you to all of our gracious hosts this week, and to everyone who attended these events.

**Photo credit: @PhilScott4VT & @Thunder_Truck**
We hope to see you at our events next week, including the [gubernatorial forum on Innovation and Technology](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=3f32d11bfc&e=19cebfe173), the [Vermont Community Development Association’s Fall Conference](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=c837b7e5d7&e=19cebfe173), the [WCAX](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=1a60568476&e=19cebfe173) and [Vermont Press Association](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=5b63e844fe&e=19cebfe173) debates, and our [Meet and Greet in Chittenden](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=4ebbea727d&e=19cebfe173). Stay up-to-date on our upcoming events [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=18bf1de402&e=19cebfe173).
***
**ICYMI: PHIL IN THE NEWS**

**_The only candidate with comprehensive plan to address our opiate epidemic_**
“Given the emergent nature of our heroin epidemic and Phil’s comprehensive plan to address it, makes clear Phil Scott is best prepared to manage our ongoing public health crisis.” – Thomas Joseph, Wilmington (Source: Caledonian Record: [Full Letter](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=e5331b617b&e=19cebfe173))

**_The experience to lead_**
“Please get out and vote for the right candidate with the experience to lead — Phil Scott.” – Marcia Guyette, Waterbury Center (Source: Rutland Herald: [Full Letter](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=24d6ce856a&e=19cebfe173))

**_Phil: Enough is enough when it comes to overtaxing Vermonters_**
“I don’t believe that we should be taxing our way out of this. We don’t need to be raising taxes on already overburdened Vermonters. At this time, we can’t afford to.” (Source: Times Argus: [Full Story](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=886bc1da3a&e=19cebfe173))
***
**ICYMI: THIS WEEK’S #GOODREADS**

**_A vote for Phil Scott is a vote for positive change_**
Throughout this election, we’ve worked to ensure Vermonters understand what is at stake and the **stark contrast between how Phil will lead Vermont toward a more prosperous future, and his opponent’s plans to continue the practices of her democratic predecessor**. Danville resident Lisa Carlson highlights that contrast so well in her op-ed in the Caledonian Record this week, that we wanted to share it [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=8888a366f4&e=19cebfe173). Describing the result of one-party rule in Montpelier, Ms. Carlson highlights a pattern of spending beyond our means, and a lack of focus on the economy. **“What you won’t see during the 2016 session is any bill addressing tax relief for middle class Vermonters or small businesses. You also won’t see any bills that would help grow the Vermont economy.”** In contrast, she highlights Phil’s commitment to do the opposite, writing, **“****As Governor, Phil Scott will make the economy his number one priority. He will also continue looking for practical and affordable ways to conserve our resources, help the most vulnerable Vermonters, improve our schools, make college more affordable, and make this state a better place to live for all Vermonters.”** Thank you for your support Lisa, Phil will not let you down. Read her full article [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=df5e6b356d&e=19cebfe173).

**_Free college? Not so fast._**
**_Seven Days_** takes look at Phil’s opponent’s plan for “free” college, finding it’s not likely to be so free, as Phil has pointed out. A lot of promises are made during the campaign, but we hope you will read the article [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=d34ac455c5&e=19cebfe173) and consider if this is something Vermonters can truly afford. The article notes, "**Minter's plan doesn't seem to be generating much vocal support, even among those in her own party**." It also points out that Phil has been accurate in noting that the tax on banks that will fund this plan will translate to higher fees for Vermonters. "Scott, Minter's Republican opponent, said at last week's debate that he opposed increasing the tax because banks would simply pass the expense to their customers.” That's true according to Chris D'Elia of the Vermont Bankers Association. "’**[Banks] can't absorb $2 to $3 million without some consequence,’ [D’Elia] said, suggesting layoffs, branch closures, fewer loans and less charitable giving would be likely.** " Read the full article [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=1e7f3f6eea&e=19cebfe173).

**_Phil Scott: Life at Full Throttle_**
We talk a lot about Phil’s public service and what he’ll do for you as Governor. But Phil is also a small business owner and a racecar driver. _Vermont Sports_ gives a great look at Phil’s life on the track and in the Statehouse in “Phil Scott: Life at Full Throttle,” which you can read [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=80ebd04dad&e=19cebfe173). In the wide-ranging profile, Phil shares more on what inspired him to racing, business and public service, and how outdoor recreation fits into his economic plans and perspective. “**How has [Scott’s] success as a snowmobile racer, a racecar driver and his hours in the saddle of his bicycles (he also mountain bikes, kayaks, snowshoes and Nordic skis, among other sports,) shaped his view of Vermont and his vision for the state as governor? To his very core, he says.** ‘When I think about Vermont and those parts of our DNA that make this state special, almost everything we’re known for revolves around our outdoors, our beauty and our spirit... that’s why we need to protect it, but also utilize it to our fullest advantage.’” Read the full article [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=65cd7ba605&e=19cebfe173) and learn more about Phil’s background [HERE](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=cb0fb3cdc3&e=19cebfe173).
*******
Thanks to everyone for continuing to follow and support our campaign. Be sure to follow along with the campaign’s activities every day – and share your thoughts – [@PhilScott4VT](http://philscott.us13.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=3dee531209&e=19cebfe173) and [https://www.facebook.com/PhilScottforVermont/](http://philscott.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=bbb0245bb16226cd6570f0156&id=fa4632879e&e=19cebfe173).

Sincerely,
Team Scott
